## Data Validity

- Source completeness semantics (S2 activity.csv): an absent row means the incident had not closed as of the extract (still open), not an export/logging gap. Evidence: (a) zero orphan rows in S2 (rules out a key-matching defect), (b) all 11 absent incidents show 205-412 hours elapsed since opened as of the extract timestamp — far beyond the closed-incident medians for their own severity (e.g., closed sev1 medians run 18-28h; the missing sev1 cases are already 200-360h old and still counting), (c) the single manual-week absent case (INC0145, sev3) shows the same signature (412h elapsed, an outlier even for sev3), consistent with "still open," not "processing lag." This evidence discriminates between the two live readings (unresolved vs. export gap); it does not discriminate a third reading ("event never truly opened") which is not live here since S1's `opened_at` field is 100% populated and independently corroborated by staffing.csv's daily open counts.
